instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Are the 20s Considered Early to Buy Term Insurance?
As life progresses, financial responsibilities start adding up. When you are young, you are relatively carefree but when you settle down, get married, and start a family, your financial needs multiply. In such situations, in the case of your premature demise, your dependents suffer a considerable financial loss. Thus, a term insurance plan comes to your family’s rescue as it compensates them for the financial loss that they suffer.
While term insurance at later stages of life makes sense, is it wise to buy the plan in your 20s?
Buying term insurance in your 20s – is it too early?
When you are in your 20s, you are usually healthy and free from lifestyle illnesses or major medical complications. As such, death due to medical complications seems like a far-off idea. Given this fact, many young adults avoid buying a term plan in their 20s. But what about sudden accidents? Are you insured against such contingencies?
Accidental deaths are sudden and unexpected. They can also cause a financial loss to your family irrespective of your age. Moreover, sudden medical contingencies can also lead to unforeseen deaths, the pandemic being the common example.
To cover such unforeseen contingencies, buying term insurance, even in your 20s, is a good idea.
Benefits of buying term insurance in your 20s
Here are some term insurance benefits that you get when you buy the plan in your 20s –
Complete protection
Even though you are healthy, an unfortunate incident can prove financially distressing for your family when you are not around. When you buy a term plan in your 20s you can secure coverage at an earlier age. This coverage protects your family’s financial needs in the case of your premature demise due to an illness or injury.
Moreover, when you are young and healthy, chances are that you don’t have any pre-existing medical conditions. This would allow you to opt for a high sum assured, add as many riders to the policy and get a comprehensive scope of coverage without restrictions or limitations. You can use the term insurance calculator and find an optimal sum assured and then choose the coverage without any limitations.
Lower premiums
The premiums of a term insurance policy primarily depend on your age. When you are young the premium would be low. You can lock in a lower premium that continues throughout the policy tenure, even as you grow old.
Moreover, at a younger age, when you are free from medical complications, there’s no loading on the premium. This also brings down the premium and makes it affordable.
Emergency planning
Emergency planning is an integral part of financial planning, necessary to keep your investments safe from emergencies. A term insurance plan serves as a tool for emergency planning. It covers premature deaths and takes care of your family, financially, in your absence.
Thus, when you are not around, your family can use the benefit received from a term insurance policy to take care of their financial needs. This leaves your investments and assets remain secure so that your family can use them for their financial goals.
Debt management
A term insurance policy also helps in managing your debt in your absence. With the benefit received from the term plan, your family can pay off your debts in case you are not around and your debts are unpaid.
Moreover, you can choose mortgage redemption plans which are term plans tied to a specific loan. The sum assured of the plan reduces regularly as your loan reduces. In your absence, the remaining part of the loan is repaid by the plan so that the debt burden doesn’t fall on your family’s head.
Thus, for managing your debt and ensuring its repayment even in your absence, a term insurance plan makes sense.
Tax benefits
You can get tax benefits on the premium that you pay for the term insurance coverage. The premium would qualify as a deduction under Section 80C up to Rs.1.5 lakhs. This deduction would lower your tax liability and help you save more.
The bottom line
Death doesn’t factor in your age. it can strike anytime and all you can do to avoid its financial repercussions is make provisions early on. A term insurance plan helps you provide against premature death. It makes sense even in your 20s because of the various term insurance benefits that you can avail of. So, do not avoid the term plan even when you are young. Use the term insurance calculator and choose an adequate sum assured for complete protection.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Note – KW term insurance age has not been used

Are the 20s Considered Early to Buy Term Insurance?
As life progresses, financial responsibilities start adding up. When you are young, you are relatively carefree but when you settle down, get married, and start a family, your financial needs multiply. In such situations, in the case of your premature demise, your dependents suffer a considerable financial loss. Thus, a term insurance plan may come to your family’s rescue as it helps in compensating them for the financial loss that they suffer.
While term insurance at later stages of life makes sense, is it wise to buy the plan in your 20s?
Buying term insurance in your 20s – is it too early?
When you are in your 20s, you are likely to be more healthy. Given this fact, many young adults avoid buying a term plan in their 20s. But what about sudden accidents? Are you insured against such contingencies?
Accidental deaths are sudden and unexpected. They can also cause a financial loss to your family irrespective of your age. Moreover, sudden medical contingencies can also lead to unforeseen deaths, the pandemic being the common example.
To cover such unforeseen contingencies, buying term insurance, even in your 20s, is preferable .
Benefits of buying term insurance in your 20s
Here are some term insurance benefits that you get when you buy the plan in your 20s –
1. Complete protection
Even though you are healthy, an unfortunate incident can prove financially distressing for your family when you are not around. When you buy a term plan in your 20s you can secure coverage at an earlier age. This coverage protects your family’s financial needs in the case of your premature demise due to an illness or injury. Moreover, when you are young and healthy, chances are that you don’t have any pre- existing medical conditions. This may allow you to opt for a high sum assured, and get a comprehensive scope of coverage without much restrictions or limitations. You can use the term insurance calculator and find an optimal sum assured and then choose the coverage. 2. Affordable premiums
The premiums of a term insurance policy primarily depend on your age. When you are young the premium would be relatively low. You can lock in a lower premium that continues throughout the policy tenure, even as you grow older. Moreover, at a younger age, when you are free from medical complications, there’s no loading on the premium. This also brings down the premium and makes it affordable. 3. Emergency planning
Emergency planning is an integral part of financial planning, necessary to keep your investments safe for emergencies. A term insurance plan serves as a tool for emergency planning. It covers premature deaths and takes care of your family, financially, in your absence. Thus, when you are not around, your family can use the benefit received from a term insurance policy to take care of their financial needs. This leaves your investments and
assets remain secure so that your family can use them for their financial goals. 4. Debt management
A term insurance policy also helps in managing your debt in your absence. With the benefit received from the term plan, your family can pay off your debts in case you are not around and your debts are unpaid. Moreover, you can choose mortgage redemption plans which are term plans tied to a specific loan. The sum assured of the plan reduces regularly as your loan reduces. In your absence, the remaining part of the loan is repaid by the plan so that the debt burden doesn’t fall on your family’s head. Thus, for managing your debt and ensuring its repayment even in your absence, a term insurance plan makes sense. 5. Tax benefits
You can get tax benefits on the premium that you pay for the term insurance coverage. The premium would qualify as a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 up to Rs.1.5 lakhs and subject to provisions stated in the Act. This deduction would lower your tax liability and help you save more.
The bottom line
Death doesn’t factor in your age. it can strike anytime and all you can do to avoid its financial repercussions is make provisions early on. A term insurance plan helps you provide against premature death. It makes sense even in your 20s because of the various term insurance benefits that you can avail of. So, do consider term plan even when you are young. Use the term insurance calculator and choose an adequate sum assured for complete protection.
